Here are comprehensive unit tests for the `parse_csv_line` function using pytest style, covering happy path, edge cases, and error cases:

```python
import pytest
from your_module import parse_csv_line  # Replace 'your_module' with the actual module name

def test_happy_path_simple():
    """Test basic CSV line parsing without quotes."""
    assert parse_csv_line("a,b,c") == ["a", "b", "c"]

def test_happy_path_with_quotes():
    """Test CSV line parsing with quoted fields."""
    assert parse_csv_line('a,"b,c",d') == ["a", "b,c", "d"]

def test_happy_path_with_spaces():
    """Test CSV line parsing with spaces around commas."""
    assert parse_csv_line(' a , " b " , c ') == [" a ", " b ", " c "]

def test_empty_line():
    """Test parsing an empty line."""
    assert parse_csv_line("") == [""]

def test_single_field():
    """Test parsing a line with a single field."""
    assert parse_csv_line("single") == ["single"]

def test_quoted_single_field():
    """Test parsing a line with a single quoted field."""
    assert parse_csv_line('"single"') == ["single"]

def test_empty_fields():
    """Test parsing a line with empty fields."""
    assert parse_csv_line(",,") == ["", "", ""]

def test_quoted_empty_fields():
    """Test parsing a line with quoted empty fields."""
    assert parse_csv_line('"","",') == ["", "", ""]

def test_commas_in_quotes():
    """Test parsing a line with commas inside quoted fields."""
    assert parse_csv_line('"a,b",c,"d,e,f"') == ["a,b", "c", "d,e,f"]

def test_quotes_in_fields():
    """Test parsing a line with quotes inside fields (not escaped)."""
    assert parse_csv_line('a"b,c') == ['a"b', "c"]

def test_leading_trailing_quotes():
    """Test parsing a line with leading or trailing quotes."""
    assert parse_csv_line('"a,b"') == ["a,b"]
    assert parse_csv_line('a"b"') == ['a"b"']

def test_multiple_consecutive_commas():
    """Test parsing a line with multiple consecutive commas."""
    assert parse_csv_line("a,,,b") == ["a", "", "", "b"]

def test_unescaped_quotes():
    """Test parsing a line with unescaped quotes in the middle of fields."""
    assert parse_csv_line('a"b,c') == ['a"b', "c"]

def test_quotes_at_start_and_end():
    """Test parsing a line with quotes at start and end of the line."""
    assert parse_csv_line('"a,b"') == ["a,b"]
    assert parse_csv_line('a,"b,c"') == ["a", "b,c"]

def test_line_with_only_commas():
    """Test parsing a line with only commas."""
    assert parse_csv_line(",,,,") == ["", "", "", "", ""]

def test_line_with_only_quotes():
    """Test parsing a line with only quotes."""
    assert parse_csv_line('""') == [""]
    assert parse_csv_line('"",""') == ["", ""]

def test_mixed_quoted_and_unquoted():
    """Test parsing a line with mixed quoted and unquoted fields."""
    assert parse_csv_line('a,"b,c",d,e,"f,g"') == ["a", "b,c", "d", "e", "f,g"]

def test_odd_number_of_quotes():
    """Test parsing a line with an odd number of quotes (unclosed quote)."""
    # This is technically invalid CSV, but we should test how our function handles it
    assert parse_csv_line('a,"b,c') == ["a", 'b,c']  # The function will treat the last quote as part of the field

def test_whitespace_preservation():
    """Test that whitespace is preserved in fields."""
    assert parse_csv_line(' a , " b " , c ') == [" a ", " b ", " c "]

def test_newline_in_quoted_field():
    """Test parsing a line with newline in quoted field (though this is a single line parser)."""
    # Note: This is a single-line parser, so newlines in the input would be part of the field
    assert parse_csv_line('"a\nb",c') == ["a\nb", "c"]

def test_special_characters():
    """Test parsing a line with special characters."""
    assert parse_csv_line('a,b@c.com,"d,e
